hero.subtitle: Sofia. Bulgarian and German military surplus, cut into rare civilian pieces.

manifest.body: Bulgarcamo works from Cold War military surplus — tarpaulins, canvas bags, sometimes uniforms — sourced in Bulgaria. Each piece is cut from the original textile and sewn in Sofia. When the material runs out, the piece leaves the catalogue.

motifs_section.title: Two camouflages, one raw material
motifs_section.intro: Two prints sourced from Bulgarian and German military textile, reworked into everyday pieces and EDC accessories.

frogskin.name: Frogskin
frogskin.subtitle: Жабешка кожа
frogskin.provenance: Coveralls for airborne and chemical troops.
frogskin.description: Bright green spots and brown rain straits on a grey background. Designed at the height of the Cold War, possibly to confuse NATO forces. Later reissued for border patrols.

splinter_1991.name: Splinter 1991
splinter_1991.provenance: Universal uniform of the Bulgarian Armed Forces.
splinter_1991.description: Angular dark green and dark brown shapes over olive, crossed by thin rain straits. Issued widely until the early two-thousands.

dpm.name: Bulgarian DPM
dpm.provenance: Adopted after the fall of the Warsaw Pact.
dpm.description: Irregular black, brown and green shapes over khaki. Modelled on British DPM. Marks the Atlantic turn of the Bulgarian Army.

flecktarn.name: German Flecktarn
flecktarn.description: Five-colour speckle — dark green, light green, black, red-brown, khaki — on a light base. The European reference for temperate-zone camouflage.

catalogue.title: All pieces
catalogue.intro: Complete inventory of the current collection — EDC accessories in two camouflages. Filter by pattern to explore each series.
catalogue.footer_note: Pricing and availability on request. Small runs, unique pieces.

sur_mesure.title: Every piece, to your measurements
sur_mesure.body: Every catalogue piece can be made to measure. Sizing adjusted to your body, choice of pattern among the available camouflages, finishes and linings on request. Bulgarcamo accepts individual orders — garments, EDC accessories or home pieces. Lead times and pricing on request, depending on complexity and material availability.

product.atelier_note: Cut and sewn in Sofia by a neighbourhood seamstress. Natural cotton lining where available.

paragraphs: Bulgarcamo works from Cold War military surplus — tarpaulins, canvas bags, sometimes uniforms — sourced in Bulgaria. The patterns are not chosen for their aesthetic but for their trace: existing materials, dated, identifiable, irreproducible.
The first release works with two of them — German Flecktarn and Bulgarian Splinter. Each piece is cut from the original textile and sewn in Sofia, by a neighbourhood seamstress.
Quantity depends on what the surplus allows — a few pieces each season, sometimes a single example. When the material runs out, the piece leaves the catalogue and stays there, marked as withdrawn.

What was an object of war becomes a civilian piece, without making a manifesto of the gesture. There is nothing to imitate, nothing to print, nothing to reproduce. There is a material that already exists, fifty or sixty years old, and a hand that takes from it to make something else.

pro.title: Sourcing & collaborations
pro.body: Bulgarcamo works with fashion designers, studios and brands seeking materials with distinctive patterns. Small runs, samples, custom sourcing — on request.

contact.title: Write to us
contact.intro: To acquire a piece, request a made-to-measure work, or collaborate. We reply within 48 working hours.

not_found.title: This page has been withdrawn.
not_found.body: The piece or page you were looking for is no longer at this address. The catalogue changes with each new sourcing — the material may have run out.
